stages.title: Design
stages.emptyState: Drag ideas here to trigger design phase

stages.title: Validation
stages.emptyState: Landing page live, traffic running, signups tracked here

projects.name: POLabel
projects.topCompetitors: FyreTrail, Multi-Label Barcodes, SKUSavvy receiving flows, and full inventory suites like Fabrikator/Katana. The wedge is not generic label printing; it is printing labels directly from open Shopify purchase orders and received quantities.
projects.pain: Shopify merchants lost the simple Stocky-era ability to print barcode labels straight from a purchase order while receiving inventory, forcing cross-checking and separate label runs every shipment.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Receiver opens a Shopify PO, checks items off as they arrive, then has to switch into a separate barcode-label workflow or third-party app, manually match SKUs/quantities, and print labels after the fact instead of directly from the PO they are actively receiving.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This happens on every inbound shipment. The operator already has the PO context in front of them, but the system breaks the moment they need labels. That creates context switching, quantity mistakes, and extra clicks at the exact point where speed matters.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: POLabel
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A tiny Shopify utility that prints barcode labels directly from purchase orders or received quantities.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify merchants with physical inventory, especially boutiques and multi-SKU retailers receiving stock weekly.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: receive PO -> confirm delivered quantities -> jump to separate barcode flow -> manually find the same items again -> print labels -> walk back to receiving.
WHY IT RECURS: Every vendor shipment repeats it; the pain scales with SKU count and partial receipts.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) Feature Request: Print Barcode Labels Directly from Purchase Orders (like Stocky) — https://community.shopify.com/t/feature-request-print-barcode-labels-directly-from-purchase-orders-like-stocky/622687
2) New Purchase Order Process — https://community.shopify.com/t/new-purchase-order-process/629542
3) Stocky App Going Away after August 31 2026 — https://community.shopify.com/t/stocky-app-going-away-after-august-31-2026/587292
4) Feature Request: Add Cost/Price/Margin Display to Purchase Order Receiving — https://community.shopify.com/t/feature-request-add-cost-price-margin-display-to-purchase-order-receiving/587455
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "One critical feature missing from the new Shopify PO & Transfers experience is the ability to print barcode labels directly from a purchase order."
- "When receiving inventory, being able to select items from a PO and immediately print labels is a huge time-saver."
- "Shopify has completely changed the process to receive a purchase order and complicated it for no apparently reason."
- "I am concerned about hearing that the Stocky App is going away this year... print price labels directly from the PO..."
- "With Stocky being discontinued in August 2026, the native Shopify Purchase Order system needs a critical update to match its functionality."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: open the PO in Shopify, then use a separate barcode app / stock app / manual label run to print labels.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: duplicate lookup, cross-referencing errors, slower receiving, and lost trust in quantity accuracy.
COMPETITORS: FyreTrail, Multi-Label Barcodes, SKUSavvy, Fabrikator/Katana receiving.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: public pricing is inconsistent or buried, and most tools bundle label printing into broader inventory products rather than a single PO-native utility.
WHY NOW: Stocky sunset plus Shopify PO workflow changes created a fresh migration window in 2026.
WEDGE: stay brutally narrow — print labels from PO lines and received quantities only.
VALIDATION ANGLE: recruit 5 stores from the Shopify threads and offer a white-glove pilot that cuts clicks during receiving.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: direct outreach to posters in the Shopify Community threads and Shopify app-store keyword traffic around Stocky sunset.
RISK FLAGS: Shopify could eventually add this natively; hardware/printer variety adds edge cases.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API for purchase orders / inventory context, existing barcode templates / printer integrations, and a tiny app surface inside admin. No dashboard required.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: It saves time inside a recurring physical workflow and restores a function merchants are explicitly asking Shopify to bring back. If it shaves even a few minutes per receipt, it earns its keep quickly for stores receiving inventory every week.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: The low end is manual relabeling and cross-reference pain. The high end is buying a broader inventory tool just to regain one missing receiving action. That is exactly the kind of gap a sharp one-function app can fill.

projects.name: APForward
projects.topCompetitors: Sufio, Order Automator, Wholesale Gorilla workarounds, email forwarding hacks, and custom Shopify Flow/webhook builds. The wedge is much narrower: just route invoices and reminder emails to the correct A/P contact per customer.
projects.pain: Shopify B2B sellers often send invoices to the buyer who placed the order instead of the payables contact who actually pays, so teams maintain off-platform spreadsheets and manually forward every reminder.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Wholesale merchant creates an order in Shopify, Shopify sends the invoice to the purchaser's email, staff checks a spreadsheet for the real A/P contact, then manually forwards invoices and later reminder emails to that separate address.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This recurs on every B2B order for customers where purchasing and payables are different people. The work is repetitive, operationally annoying, and easy to mess up, especially when reminders go to the wrong inbox and payment gets delayed.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: APForward
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A tiny Shopify B2B app that stores an alternate A/P email per customer and automatically routes invoice + reminder emails there.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify Plus / wholesale merchants using net terms with repeat B2B buyers.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: order placed by buyer -> invoice sent to buyer email -> staff checks off-platform sheet for A/P email -> manually forwards invoice -> manually forwards every reminder again.
WHY IT RECURS: Every net-terms order repeats the same routing mistake because the buyer and payables contact are often different humans.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) How to send automatically invoices and payment reminders to alternate A/P email through Shopify? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-to-send-automatically-invoices-and-payment-reminders-to-alternate-a-p-email-through-shopify/327432
2) How can I manage partial payments for wholesale customers on Shopify? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-can-i-manage-partial-payments-for-wholesale-customers-on-shopify/235601
3) Tag orders that are 30 days overdue with Flow — https://community.shopify.com/t/tag-orders-that-are-30-days-overdue-with-flow/295506
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "Shopify currently sends invoices and payment reminders to the email address used for order placement. However, some customers have a different A/P email address."
- "We have to track the alternate email addresses with a spreadsheet outside of Shopify and manually send invoices and payment reminders."
- "They require that I send them statements at month end with the amounts overdue and reflecting the amounts paid."
- "I want to filter those orders by a tag on the orders page so our accounts receivable team can easily know which are 30 days past due."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: spreadsheet of alternate payables emails, inbox forwarding, manual reminders, or custom scripts/flows.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: wrong-recipient reminders, duplicated labor, stale contact data, and slower collections.
COMPETITORS: Sufio, Order Automator, custom Flow/webhook builds, accounting suite syncs.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: pricing is usually bundled into broader invoicing/automation products rather than sold as a single routing utility; public pricing varies and is not the core wedge here.
WHY NOW: Shopify B2B adoption is up, but this basic routing gap is still unresolved in core.
WEDGE: one job only — store the A/P address and send the invoice/reminder to the right inbox every time.
VALIDATION ANGLE: install for 5 wholesale stores, import current A/P contacts, and measure reduction in manual forwards and payment lag.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community B2B threads plus outreach to stores using Wholesale Gorilla or net terms.
RISK FLAGS: some merchants may solve it in accounting tools; buyer records may need clean customer-company mapping.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API for orders/customers/companies, metafields for alternate A/P email storage, and transactional email sending. Feasible without building a broader receivables product.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: Collections speed touches cash flow directly. If a small app removes repetitive forwarding and gets reminders to the person who can actually approve payment, the ROI is obvious for wholesale teams.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: Today merchants use spreadsheets, forwarding rules, or bloated invoicing suites. None of those are a clean answer to the single missing action they keep describing: send the invoice to the right payables inbox automatically.

projects.name: Net30Ping
projects.topCompetitors: Shopify Flow DIY setups, generic dunning apps, Sufio reminders, and accounting-suite reminder tooling. The wedge is specifically overdue monitoring/reminders for Shopify payment-terms orders without Flow gymnastics.
projects.pain: Merchants using Shopify payment terms want a simple way to tag overdue invoices and send repeat reminders until paid, but today they stitch together brittle Flow chains or check overdue orders manually.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Merchant sends a payment-terms invoice, waits for due date, then either manually checks for overdue orders or builds a long Shopify Flow stack with waits, tags, checks, and repeat reminder steps to keep nudging until payment arrives.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: The pain recurs on every overdue invoice. It is not strategic work; it is repetitive collections hygiene. The current DIY automation is brittle, hard to maintain, and still leaves teams asking how to identify and act on overdue orders cleanly.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: Net30Ping
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A one-function Shopify utility that tags overdue payment-term orders and sends repeat reminders until paid.
TARGET BUYER: Wholesale / B2B Shopify merchants extending net terms.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: invoice sent on terms -> due date passes -> staff manually checks or builds Flow chains -> order gets tagged overdue -> reminders are sent inconsistently until payment lands.
WHY IT RECURS: Every unpaid net-terms invoice creates the same follow-up problem.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) Tag orders that are 30 days overdue with Flow — https://community.shopify.com/t/tag-orders-that-are-30-days-overdue-with-flow/295506
2) Shopify Flow: daily “until paid” reminders + 5-days-before due loops / scheduled time / order context — https://community.shopify.com/t/shopify-flow-daily-until-paid-reminders-5-days-before-due-loops-scheduled-time-order-context/572816
3) How can I manage partial payments for wholesale customers on Shopify? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-can-i-manage-partial-payments-for-wholesale-customers-on-shopify/235601
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "Can someone help me configure Flow to tag orders that are past due by 30 days?"
- "I want to filter those orders by a tag on the orders page so our accounts receivable team can easily know which are 30 days past due."
- "It works, but it's messy. Because Flow doesn't really 'loop,' I had to stack a long chain of steps: wait 1 day -> check if paid -> if not, send reminder -> repeat."
- "My plan... Scheduled time (daily) -> fetch up to 100 orders with the overdue tag that are still unpaid..."
- "I also sometimes receive partial payments by bank transfer on some of the invoices."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: manual checks, brittle multi-flow recipes, tags, code steps, and generic reminder tools.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: operator effort, easy breakage, limited visibility, and recurring maintenance for something that should be trivial.
COMPETITORS: Shopify Flow DIY, generic dunning apps, accounting tool reminders.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: market alternatives for basic dunning/reminder apps are often low-cost, which supports a small sharp product; differentiation must come from Shopify payment-terms specificity and dead-simple setup.
WHY NOW: more merchants are using Shopify B2B/payment terms, while forum threads show the native overdue workflow is still awkward.
WEDGE: no suite, no ledger, no analytics — just detect overdue orders and keep reminders going until paid.
VALIDATION ANGLE: offer a plug-and-play install to merchants already struggling with Flow; prove they can replace a multi-step automation with one toggle.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community posts about overdue orders and Shopify Flow templates for B2B collections.
RISK FLAGS: could be imitated by Flow templates or invoicing apps; needs clean guardrails for reminder cadence and opt-outs.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API / GraphQL payment terms fields, order tags or metafields, scheduled jobs, and transactional email sending. Very small product surface.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: It removes a recurring A/R chore and replaces fragile Flow logic with one clear behavior. For stores with even modest wholesale volume, a cheap specialized reminder tool is easier to justify than maintaining custom automation.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: The current options are DIY Flow chains, generic reminder products, or pushing the job into accounting software. None are as simple as a purpose-built overdue tagger + reminder engine inside Shopify.

projects.name: BinSlip
projects.topCompetitors: DIY Order Printer template edits, Shopify Flow/metafield workarounds, adjacent pick-list apps like PickPilot, document tools like Order Printer Pro, and broader warehouse systems. The wedge is narrower than all of them: show bin locations and scan-friendly pick data on packing slips without custom Liquid or order-level data copying.
projects.pain: Shopify merchants keep hacking metafields, Flow, and Order Printer templates just to show bin locations and pick/scan data on packing slips, turning basic picking into a brittle DIY system.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Merchant stores bin data on products or variants, then has to make that data printable by editing Order Printer templates, testing Liquid access to the right metafields, and often adding Flow automations that copy location data onto orders before slips are printed for pickers.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: The picker needs location context at the exact moment they are walking shelves, but Shopify turns that into a multi-layer setup involving data storage, template logic, and automation. The result is brittle fulfillment infrastructure around a very ordinary warehouse need.

details.title: Evidence
details.text: Primary-source pain appears in multiple Shopify Community threads where merchants explicitly ask how to get bin locations or related scan data onto packing slips and describe Order Printer migrations or Flow copy hacks as part of the workaround. See the dedicated Direct Evidence Links and Exact Quotes sections below.

details.title: One-line Opportunity
details.text: A tiny Shopify fulfillment utility that makes packing slips and pick sheets reliably show bin locations and scan-friendly line-item data without custom Liquid, metafield copy hacks, or Flow gymnastics.

details.title: Target Buyer
details.text: Shopify merchants with physical inventory and shelf/bin organization—especially small warehouses, retail backrooms, and founder-led ops teams that still print packing slips or pick sheets during fulfillment.

details.title: Exact Painful Workflow
details.text: Store bin location on the product or variant -> realize the picker cannot see it on the packing slip by default -> edit Order Printer Liquid -> discover the needed metafield is not available in the right context -> add Shopify Flow to copy variant/product data onto the order -> re-test every template change -> print slips -> hope the field still renders during the next fulfillment batch.

details.title: Why It Recurs
details.text: This is not a one-time setup annoyance. Every new order needs the same shelf/bin context at pick time, and every template migration, metafield schema change, or Order Printer change can break the workaround again. The pain compounds with SKU count and fulfillment frequency.

details.title: Direct Evidence Links
details.text:
1) Copying variant metafield data to order metafield using Shopify Flow — https://community.shopify.com/t/copying-variant-metafield-data-to-order-metafield-using-shopify-flow/415644
2) Metafields on Shopify Packing Slip — https://community.shopify.com/t/metafields-on-shopify-packing-slip/237365
3) New Order Printer to Show Product line item Meta Fields — https://community.shopify.com/t/new-order-printer-to-show-product-line-item-meta-fields/323267

details.title: Exact Quotes / Source Excerpts
details.text:
- “Currently trying to add bin location data to packing slips for when I’m shipping products...”
- “...the problem is auto-populating it when an order is created from variant.metafields.stock.bin.”
- “Is there a way for me to add a line on my packing slips for location of items? Example: Bin Location?”
- “Also is there a way to add barcodes to the packing slips as well? I see other owners scanning packing slips all the time but i can’t seem to figure out how.”
- “We created a packing slip template to show Bin Locations so we can easily find products when picking.”
- “We Installed the New Order Printer App... But when we migrate our templates, it won’t show the product meta fields anymore.”

details.title: Current Workaround
details.text: Merchants use product/variant metafields for bin data, then either hand-edit Order Printer templates or build Shopify Flow automations that copy those values onto order-level fields so the packing slip can render them. Some give up and use a separate pick-list app or a broader warehouse tool.

details.title: Why Workaround Is Bad
details.text: The workaround is fragile, technical, and easy to regress. It mixes data modeling, template code, and automation logic for a problem that should feel like a checkbox. When it breaks, pickers lose location context immediately, which slows walking, increases search time, and creates hidden dependency on whoever built the hack.

details.title: Competitors
details.text:
- DIY: Order Printer template edits + metafields + Shopify Flow
- Adjacent app: PickPilot – Pick List
- Adjacent document app: Order Printer Pro
- Broader category: pick/pack apps and WMS suites that include location-aware picking as one feature among many

details.title: Competitor Pricing
details.text: Public pricing I could verify: PickPilot – Pick List lists $4.99/month on the Shopify App Store, and Order Printer Pro lists $10/month. For broader WMS/pick-pack suites, pricing is often bundled, tiered, sales-led, or simply not positioned around this exact use case. There does not appear to be obvious public pricing for a direct “bin locations on packing slips only” competitor, which reinforces the wedge.

details.title: Why Now
details.text: Shopify merchants are already reworking document workflows because of Order Printer changes and related tooling churn. That makes fragile packing-slip hacks more visible and more annoying right now. At the same time, even small merchants are adopting more organized bin-based fulfillment as SKU counts rise, so the need for location-aware slips shows up earlier than before.

details.title: Wedge
details.text: Do one thing extremely well: make the printed packing slip or pick sheet always include the right bin/location and scan-friendly line data, without requiring the merchant to understand Liquid, metafield access patterns, or Flow data-copy tricks.

details.title: Validation Angle
details.text: Start with merchants already asking Shopify Community questions about bin locations and packing slips. Offer a white-glove install that replaces their current template/Flow setup. The validation question is simple: will they pay to stop touching Liquid and stop debugging this workflow?

details.title: First Traffic Source
details.text: Shopify Community threads about packing slips, bin locations, barcodes, and Order Printer migration pain. Secondary channel: Shopify App Store search intent around “packing slip”, “pick list”, “bin location”, and “Order Printer”.

details.title: Risk Flags
details.text:
- Some merchants may decide a generic pick-list app is “good enough”
- Shopify could improve Order Printer/metafield support natively
- Printer/template edge cases may create support load
- If the app has to rely on brittle document extension surfaces, maintenance cost could creep up

details.title: Kill Criteria
details.text: Kill or demote if: (1) outreach to 10-15 relevant merchants produces little urgency, (2) most merchants are satisfied by a cheap generic pick-list app, (3) Shopify native templating already solves the problem cleanly enough, or (4) implementation requires too much per-store template handholding to stay a small software business.

details.title: Final Verdict
details.text: KEEP. BinSlip now looks like a legitimate micro-SaaS wedge: the pain is real, recurring, operational, and documented in primary-source threads. It is narrow enough for a fast MVP, distinct from bloated warehouse software, and credible as a paid “remove the hack” utility—provided early validation confirms merchants will pay for reliability, not just ask for free template help.

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API / GraphQL for product, variant, order, and metafield data; Order Printer template surface; optional Shopify Flow support for merchants who want sync triggers.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: It saves time in a recurring pick/pack loop and reduces the chance that fulfillment grinds to a halt because a custom template or Flow broke. For merchants shipping daily, buying back even a few minutes per batch—and removing fragile technical upkeep—has clear ROI.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: Today the low end is DIY Liquid plus Flow; the middle is cheap adjacent apps that do not cleanly solve the exact packing-slip/location problem; the high end is adopting a broader WMS or pick-pack suite just to expose bin data to pickers. That gap is the opportunity.

projects.name: VendorSKU
projects.topCompetitors: Metafield workarounds, spreadsheets, custom private apps, and larger stock-control suites like Pasilobus or FyreTrail. The wedge is focused: keep supplier SKU / cost fields sane and auto-fill them into Shopify purchase orders.
projects.pain: Shopify merchants cannot reliably bulk-manage supplier SKUs and related purchasing fields or auto-fill them into purchase orders, so buyers hand-maintain critical vendor data and retype it every PO.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Buyer stores supplier SKU, cost, or tax context in ad hoc places, then when creating a PO manually re-enters or cross-checks those values line by line because Shopify does not cleanly pull them through or allow practical bulk upkeep.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This repeats on every vendor order and every catalog update. The exact fields purchasing teams need to make a PO useful are the same fields Shopify merchants say are hardest to bulk maintain, which creates slow PO prep and easy ordering mistakes.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: VendorSKU
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A focused Shopify purchasing utility that bulk-manages supplier SKUs/cost fields and auto-fills them into POs.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify merchants with repeat supplier ordering and large enough catalogs that manual PO prep is painful.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: maintain supplier identifiers manually -> create PO -> re-enter or verify supplier SKU and cost fields by hand -> catch errors after suppliers question the PO.
WHY IT RECURS: Every catalog maintenance cycle and every purchase order repeats the same field-management problem.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) Supplier SKU and Shop SKU in Purchase Orders — https://community.shopify.com/t/supplier-sku-and-shop-sku-in-purchase-orders/247864
2) Supplier SKU, Cost, Tax % Bulk Editing — https://community.shopify.com/t/supplier-sku-cost-tax-bulk-editing/622276
3) Purchase Orders - Automatically updating cost price & Changing Currency — https://community.shopify.com/t/purchase-orders-automatically-updating-cost-price-changing-currency/253535
4) Product Supplier SKU and Shop SKU — https://community.shopify.com/t/product-supplier-sku-and-shop-sku/8427
5) Streamline Purchase Orders — Auto-fill from Catalog & Add to PO at Product Creation — https://community.shopify.com/t/streamline-purchase-orders-auto-fill-from-catalog-add-to-po-at-product-creation/615592
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "surely there is a way for this data to be pulled across from the product inventory"
- "there is still no way to bulk edit Supplier SKU, Cost and Tax percentage information so it all can only be done by hand"
- "Each product has the cost price included on each product page, so was hoping that part would automatically be pulled into the PO"
- "I want to create my own SKU... when placing an order with a supplier I know their SKU for the product"
- "allow me to search and add existing products/variants from my catalog by vendor — automatically pulling in SKU and unit cost"
CURRENT WORKAROUND: metafields, spreadsheets, manual entry, or building a private script/app.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: stale vendor data, wrong-SKU ordering risk, duplicate data entry, and too much operator time spent on basic purchasing hygiene.
COMPETITORS: spreadsheets, private apps, stock-control suites, custom integrations.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: full inventory suites are materially broader than this workflow and often overkill if the merchant only wants sane PO prep.
WHY NOW: Stocky sunset and Shopify's newer PO workflow are exposing how underbuilt native purchasing still is.
WEDGE: own the supplier-data layer for Shopify POs, not all of inventory management.
VALIDATION ANGLE: onboard merchants already complaining about supplier SKU pain and measure PO prep time + ordering accuracy before/after.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community PO threads plus app-store keywords around supplier SKU and Stocky replacement.
RISK FLAGS: some merchants will prefer all-in-one inventory tools; PO API limitations may require a narrow first release.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API / GraphQL for products, variants, inventory metadata, vendor fields, and metafields; CSV import/export surfaces; optional browser-extension style workflow if PO write surfaces stay constrained.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: Bad supplier data directly creates ordering mistakes and wasted buyer time. A small tool that keeps the right identifiers/costs ready at PO time can save enough labor and prevent enough errors to justify a modest monthly fee.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: The low end is spreadsheets and manual edits. The high end is adopting a full stock-control suite or commissioning a private app just to fix supplier fields. That gap supports a sharp utility product.

projects.name: POImport
projects.topCompetitors: Stocky workarounds, spreadsheet sync tools, custom CSV scripts, and broader purchasing/inventory apps. The wedge is simple: ingest supplier or rep CSVs and bulk-build/update Shopify purchase orders without line-by-line entry.
projects.pain: Merchants receiving supplier or sales-rep CSVs still key purchase orders into Shopify line by line because native bulk-add and CSV-import workflows are weak or missing.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Merchant gets a supplier CSV or rep-generated PO list, opens Shopify purchase orders, and then copy-pastes SKUs or manually keys hundreds of lines into the Add Products dialog because there is no clean bulk import path that matches how they already receive order data.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This pain repeats every buying cycle. The data already exists in CSV form, but operators still burn time on re-entry, deal with search popups, and risk missing or mis-keying lines when purchase orders are large or random.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: POImport
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A Shopify utility that turns supplier / rep CSVs into purchase orders and bulk line updates without manual re-entry.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify merchants and buyers who regularly place medium-to-large purchase orders from vendor spreadsheets or rep exports.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: receive CSV from rep/supplier -> open PO in Shopify -> paste/search/add products manually -> verify quantities and lines -> sometimes separately update incoming/received state.
WHY IT RECURS: Every reorder cycle, vendor drop, or replenishment batch can arrive as a file instead of a Shopify-native object.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) [Feature Request] Adding products in bulk on purchase order and collection page — https://community.shopify.com/t/feature-request-adding-products-in-bulk-on-purchase-order-and-collection-page/369146
2) Purchase order app that updates the 'Incoming' part of a product — https://community.shopify.com/t/purchase-order-app-that-updates-the-incoming-part-of-a-product/291774
3) Creating PO’s using CSV files supplied by reps — https://community.shopify.com/t/creating-po-s-using-csv-files-supplied-by-reps/252839
4) How to input received Purchase Orders via CSV file — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-to-input-received-purchase-orders-via-csv-file/324934
5) Streamline Purchase Orders — Auto-fill from Catalog & Add to PO at Product Creation — https://community.shopify.com/t/streamline-purchase-orders-auto-fill-from-catalog-add-to-po-at-product-creation/615592
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "We are frequently adding hundreds of products to the purchase order page, but there is no efficient way to do it"
- "especially when dealing with incoming orders with a high number of products (no CSV import)"
- "My sales reps email me a CSV file of a purchase order created. What is the easy way to upload this file into a PO to save time"
- "I would really like to not input 200+ items on a purchase order line by line"
- "I was wondering if there is a way to upload a file that contains all the updates"
CURRENT WORKAROUND: copy-paste SKU lists into dialogs, manual line entry, spreadsheet sync tools that do not cleanly handle POs, or broader purchasing apps.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: duplicate data entry, missed lines, slow replenishment work, and extra cleanup when incoming/received quantities must be reflected accurately.
COMPETITORS: spreadsheet connectors, Stocky workarounds, broad inventory apps, custom scripts.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: alternatives tend to bundle PO import inside broader inventory products or require custom setup rather than solving this one ingest step cleanly.
WHY NOW: Shopify merchants are actively reassessing PO workflows as Stocky capabilities shift and native PO expectations rise.
WEDGE: do one small but valuable job — make existing CSVs usable as clean Shopify PO input.
VALIDATION ANGLE: import live vendor CSVs for 5 merchants and measure how much PO creation time and re-keying disappears.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community PO threads, Stocky replacement searches, and direct outreach to merchants asking about CSV imports.
RISK FLAGS: write limitations around native POs may constrain depth; some merchants may accept spreadsheet bridges if they are already set up.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API / GraphQL for products, variants, inventory, and incoming quantity context; CSV parsing; optional browser automation or companion import flow if direct PO mutation is constrained.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: Buyers already receive structured order data; paying to skip re-entry is rational when every PO burns staff time and creates avoidable mistakes. The ROI is clearest for stores regularly ordering 100+ lines.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: Today merchants either re-key everything, abuse spreadsheet apps not built for Shopify POs, or adopt much larger inventory software. A focused importer can win by being faster and narrower.

projects.name: BufferStock
projects.topCompetitors: Merchbees Low Stock Alert, Bundleup, Stocky, and broader inventory suites like Fabrikator/Katana. The wedge is narrower: reserve thresholds plus hidden consumables/components tied to sellable SKUs, without requiring a full ERP-style setup.
projects.pain: Shopify merchants repeatedly run out of hidden packaging or safety-stock items because low-stock alerts and reorder rules are split across spreadsheets, generic alert apps, and manual purchase-order work.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Merchant watches stock levels in Shopify, keeps separate mental or spreadsheet buffer targets, manually remembers hidden supplies like envelopes/bands/packaging, then rebuilds purchase orders by hand when something falls below a threshold.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This is not a one-off setup problem. It repeats every week as fast-moving variants, reserve stock, and hidden 1:1 packaging components drift out of sync. The result is stockouts, fulfillment delays, and too many manual reorder decisions for simple replenishment rules.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: BufferStock
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A lightweight Shopify replenishment app that manages custom reserve thresholds and hidden packaging/component stock tied to sellable SKUs.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify merchants with physical inventory, especially boutiques, stationery, gift, and specialty retailers that keep safety stock and non-sellable packaging supplies.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: watch low stock manually -> check spreadsheet/tribal knowledge for true minimums -> remember hidden components like envelopes/bands -> build purchase order by hand -> still miss something and delay fulfillment.
WHY IT RECURS: Every receiving/replenishment cycle repeats it, especially for stores with recurring inbound shipments and non-sellable components.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) App for low stock/stop selling at threshold — https://community.shopify.com/t/app-for-low-stock-stop-selling-at-threshold/70829
2) What’s the best inventory app for tracking low stock and custom packaging? — https://community.shopify.com/t/whats-the-best-inventory-app-for-tracking-low-stock-and-custom-packaging/70860
3) Setting our own MOH quantities for purchasing Stocky — https://community.shopify.com/t/setting-our-own-moh-quantities-for-purchasing-stocky/45944
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "After warranty service, some products have to be kept in stock for repair purposes. Therefore, when stock falls below the threshold, the product should not be sold any more."
- "Right now we are doing it all manually, and we are usually too busy to do a good job keeping up we run out of things all the time and it leads to delays."
- "Looking for recommendations on how to force Stocky to allow us to set our own minimum quantity to keep on hand so we can stop manually having to do every purchase order."
- "Their random metrics don’t mesh with my reality of what my store needs to have on hand and what quantities I need to order in."
- "We are having the same issue."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: generic low-stock email apps, hidden products bundled into manual inventory tricks, spreadsheets, and hand-built purchase orders.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: merchants still have to remember true reserve quantities and hidden components themselves, so the alerting layer and the purchasing layer remain disconnected.
COMPETITORS: Merchbees Low Stock Alert, Bundleup, Stocky, Fabrikator, Katana.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: most pricing is bundled into either alerting tools or full inventory suites; the reserve-stock + hidden-component wedge is not sold cleanly as its own utility.
WHY NOW: Shopify merchants are still dealing with post-Stocky workflow gaps and want simpler, narrower admin tools instead of broad inventory platforms.
WEDGE: start with custom min/max thresholds, reserve-stock rules, and hidden 1:1 component depletion only.
VALIDATION ANGLE: onboard 5 stores that currently use spreadsheets for reorder buffers and measure avoided stockouts / fewer manual PO edits.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community inventory threads plus keywords around low-stock alerts, Stocky alternatives, and packaging inventory.
RISK FLAGS: some merchants may prefer broader suites; component modeling can get messy if BOMs become multi-level.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API for products, variants, inventory levels, locations, purchase-order context, and metafields to store reserve thresholds and component mappings.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: This saves merchants from the most annoying part of replenishment: remembering the real minimum, the hidden supply dependencies, and the next purchase decision. Avoiding even a few preventable stockouts or fulfillment delays makes the subscription easy to justify.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: The low end is spreadsheets, sticky-note buffer rules, and generic alerts that stop before action. The high end is buying a larger inventory suite just to solve reserve thresholds and hidden packaging dependencies. That gap is small, painful, and monetizable.

projects.name: RestockDate
projects.topCompetitors: Xeon Back In Stock Date, generic back-in-stock notification apps, Mechanic/custom metafield automations, and bespoke theme work. The wedge is not alerts after restock; it is showing and preserving expected availability dates before the item is back.
projects.pain: Shopify merchants that allow backorders or preorders still manually chase and publish expected restock dates across product pages, carts, and order confirmations, creating support load and broken promises when dates shift.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Merchant receives supplier or transfer ETA data, manually adds dates to product pages or metafields, tries to surface them on variant pages and emails, then updates the same date again when shipments slip.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This repeats for every oversold, backordered, or incoming variant. The pain is not just display; it is synchronizing one volatile date across PDPs, carts, and customer comms while keeping a record of what was promised when the order was placed.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: RestockDate
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A Shopify app that shows expected restock dates on out-of-stock variants and preserves the promised ETA in customer-facing order communication.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify merchants selling inventory with recurring supplier ETAs, preorders, or allowed overselling/backorders.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: supplier sends ETA -> merchant edits theme/metafield/product text -> customer sees date on product page -> date changes -> merchant updates it again and struggles to reflect the promised date in cart / confirmation / support replies.
WHY IT RECURS: Every delayed replenishment cycle or incoming transfer repeats it, especially for stores that oversell or preorder.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) Real Time "expected stock date" app for products — https://community.shopify.com/t/real-time-expected-stock-date-app-for-products/81497
2) Looking for an app to provide an estimated in stock date on the product variant — https://community.shopify.com/t/looking-for-an-app-to-provide-an-estimated-in-stock-date-on-the-product-variant/81541
3) Show ETA of out of stock products / Show expected arrival date — https://community.shopify.com/t/show-eta-of-out-of-stock-products-show-expected-arrival-date/86615
4) Display incoming transfer date on the new order confirmation — https://community.shopify.com/t/display-incoming-transfer-date-on-the-new-order-confirmation/20871
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "My store has several suppliers which use online inventory tracking and have an expected back in stock date which i would like to display to customers on specific product pages."
- "We are looking for an app that will be able to put an estimated in stock date next to a product variant so that out customers can see when we estimate that variant will be back in stock."
- "We would like it to link to our Purchase Orders if at all possible?"
- "Trying to find a solution to show the ETA of out-of-stock products when we allow overselling."
- "We would like to have some record of what date was displaying at the time a customer purchased."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: custom Liquid, transfer-based next_incoming_date hacks, manual metafields, spreadsheet apps, or paying for one-off theme/dev work.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: dates drift across surfaces, support has no clean audit trail, and merchants still manually chase each delay.
COMPETITORS: Xeon Back In Stock Date, generic back-in-stock apps, Mechanic automations, custom theme development.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: app-store solutions exist, but many focus on notification alerts rather than reliable ETA display + order-time promise capture.
WHY NOW: more merchants are selling through stock shortages and leaner inventories, so transparent ETA communication is now revenue-protecting, not just nice-to-have.
WEDGE: start with ETA display on PDP + variant level, cart/order confirmation capture, and simple admin updates from transfer/PO/metafield data.
VALIDATION ANGLE: install in 5 stores that oversell or preorder and measure support-ticket reduction plus conversion on out-of-stock-but-orderable SKUs.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community threads plus app-store search terms around back in stock date, expected arrival date, and preorder ETA.
RISK FLAGS: some themes can expose next_incoming_date natively; buyer trust depends on merchant date accuracy.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API for products/variants/metafields, transfer/PO-derived incoming dates where available, theme app extensions for display blocks, and email template support for order-time ETA capture.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: A store only needs a handful of delayed SKUs to create repetitive support tickets and abandoned carts. If this app helps merchants keep selling while setting clearer expectations, it pays for itself quickly.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: Today the merchant either does manual theme work, buys a broader preorder app, or relies on scattered metafields and staff memory. None cleanly solve the recurring operational task of publishing, updating, and recording promised restock dates.

projects.name: BorderPack
projects.topCompetitors: Order Printer templates, Sufio, Matrixify/Ablestar bulk editors, and custom Liquid/Mechanic workflows. The wedge is narrower: produce customs-ready commercial invoice / packing-slip data for international Shopify orders without merchants hacking templates field by field.
projects.pain: Shopify merchants shipping internationally still patch together HS codes, country-of-origin fields, line totals, and printable invoices manually because the exact data needed for customs is fragmented across product data, order data, and printer templates.

details.title: Current Manual Workflow
details.text: Merchant prepares an international order, discovers the default invoice/packing slip is missing customs fields, hacks Order Printer templates, manually fixes HS codes or line totals, and grants extra app permissions so warehouse staff can print the right document.

details.title: Workflow Friction
details.text: This repeats on every cross-border shipment. The work is detail-heavy, legally sensitive, and brittle because required fields live in different places and Shopify’s defaults do not map neatly onto customs-ready documents.

details.title: Evidence
details.text:
IDEA NAME: BorderPack
ONE-LINE OPPORTUNITY: A Shopify app that generates customs-ready commercial invoices and packing documents with HS code, COO, line total, and print-routing support for international orders.
TARGET BUYER: Shopify merchants and 3PL-connected stores shipping internationally, especially teams that rely on warehouse staff or Order Printer templates.
EXACT PAINFUL WORKFLOW: receive international order -> discover required customs fields are missing -> patch invoice template -> manually fix HS/COO/line totals -> coordinate warehouse printing permissions -> repeat every shipment.
WHY IT RECURS: Every international order needs compliant paperwork, and every template/data gap becomes repeated ops work.
DIRECT EVIDENCE LINKS:
1) Adding HS code and country of origin to packing list — https://community.shopify.com/t/adding-hs-code-and-country-of-origin-to-packing-list/70819
2) How to enter 8 digit HS codes in Shopify for European shipping — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-to-enter-8-digit-hs-codes-in-shopify-for-european-shipping/33988
3) Trying to add TOTAL line price to an Order Printer invoice that I am converting to a commercial invoice — https://community.shopify.com/t/trying-to-add-total-line-price-to-an-order-printer-invoice-that-i-am-converting-to-a-commercial-inv/34505
4) How can I view and print invoices for international orders on Shopify? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-can-i-view-and-print-invoices-for-international-orders-on-shopify/45618
5) How do I access the HS code in the Order API? — https://community.shopify.com/t/how-do-i-access-the-hs-code-in-the-order-api/46089
QUOTED / SOURCE EXCERPTS:
- "How do I change the packing slip template to add Country of Origin and HS code to the document?"
- "otherwise, we have to manually add codes each time we ship to Europe…such a time waster!"
- "UK carriers are asking for the full 8 digit HS code... I don’t fancy manually updating 8000+ products."
- "I have managed to work around adding all of the relevant details required for a legal Commercial Invoice except for adding HS CODES..."
- "for international orders they need to print an invoice for customs processes of related countries. They can not print out the invoices on our Shopify admin."
- "Our Order json just shows an empty value for the 'duties'..."
CURRENT WORKAROUND: hacked Order Printer templates, bulk-editor workarounds, staff permission changes, custom scripts, or manual customs field entry.
WHY WORKAROUND IS BAD: every missing field becomes repeated labor in a compliance-sensitive workflow, and small template mistakes can delay shipments or create customs issues.
COMPETITORS: Order Printer templates, Sufio, Matrixify, Ablestar, Mechanic/custom development.
COMPETITOR PRICING IF FINDABLE: most tools either focus on invoicing broadly or on bulk product editing; few are positioned as a narrow international-docs ops utility.
WHY NOW: cross-border shipping complexity remains high while more small merchants rely on 3PLs and staff accounts to handle fulfillment.
WEDGE: start with one job — render customs-ready international docs from Shopify order + product data, then make them printable by warehouse staff.
VALIDATION ANGLE: pilot with stores shipping to EU/UK and track time saved per international order plus reduction in manual doc edits.
FIRST TRAFFIC SOURCE: Shopify Community shipping threads and app-store terms around commercial invoice, HS code, and international order printer.
RISK FLAGS: document requirements vary by destination/carrier; some merchants may already solve pieces with 3PL software.
VERDICT: KEEP

details.title: Relevant APIs
details.text: Shopify Admin API for orders/products/variants/metafields, duties/HS-related fields where available, theme/app printing surfaces, and PDF generation for commercial invoice / packing slip exports.

details.title: Why They'd Pay
details.text: Cross-border paperwork is annoying, repeated, and tied directly to whether orders leave the warehouse cleanly. If a merchant ships internationally every week, shaving even a few minutes and reducing customs-risk friction is worth paying for.

details.title: Existing Bad Solutions
details.text: The low end is manual document editing and template hacking. The high end is buying broader invoicing or shipping software that does much more than the merchant needs. A narrow cross-border docs tool fits the gap cleanly.
